Details: Although a bench-scale study observed high removals (88 to 92 percent depending on pH) of vinyl chloride by UV irradiation, this level of performance required contact times on the order of 150 minutes. Removals were much lower (14 to 34 percent) at 30 minutes contact time [2805]. A similar study found vinyl chloride degradation by UV irradiation was enhanced with the addition of sulfite, but substantial removal still required long contact times (e.g., 100 minutes) [2806]. The contact times in these studies were significantly longer than those typically used in full-scale UV treatment of drinking water.
